combined_bound_chain: Restrict the finite Level 2 Dirac matrix to the complement of the three protected zero modes.
Use the exact finite-basis diagonal contribution and finite q/residual ranges for diagonal sign-indefinite terms.
Control sector off-diagonal blocks with Gershgorin and restricted min-max bounds.
Use the v1.3B sector-coupling operator-norm audit to distinguish norm-certified cases from finite-basis-only passes.
Square the symmetric Level 2 Dirac matrix and take the conservative complement lower bound.
Apply the monotone heat-lift inequality with Lambda^2 = 1/(4*pi).
Add only PSD curvature/profile contributions, so Weyl's lower bound cannot decrease.
Keep theorem_complete=False until the zero-mode/complement split and off-diagonal bounds are proven in the full action.

limitations: This inventory classifies the current Level 2 finite-basis proxy; it is not the full analytic H_T spectrum.
Frozen v1.0/v1.1 predictions, constants, tolerances, and mode ledgers are not changed.

assumptions: Level 2 matrix uses the existing Berger anisotropy and finite mode basis.
The protected zero-mode rows and columns are projected out by construction.
limitations: The term is exact only inside DIRAC_PROXY_LEVEL_2.
A closed analytic Berger twisted-Dirac spectrum is not derived here.
terms.lower_bound_method: exact diagonal minimum after complement restriction; min-max check after squaring
terms.matrix_structure: diagonal in the finite Dirac basis before the D^dagger D construction
terms.name: Berger / Dirac kinetic diagonal contribution
terms.operator_role: dominant signed diagonal Dirac proxy contribution before squaring

assumptions: The finite basis has bounded Hopf charges.
The Hopf coefficient is fixed before residual comparison.
limitations: The current lower bound is finite-basis bounded, not a global analytic q-bound.
terms.lower_bound_method: Weyl bound with explicit coefficient and finite q range
terms.matrix_structure: diagonal but sign-indefinite because q can have either sign in general basis choices
terms.name: Hopf twist contribution

assumptions: Boundary coefficients are those from the v1.2 action-origin scaffold.
Boundary strength is explicit and nonnegative.
limitations: The full action-level uniqueness of the complete internal action remains open.
A global analytic residual bound beyond the finite basis is not proven here.
terms.lower_bound_method: Weyl bound using the bounded residual range in the finite basis
terms.matrix_structure: diagonal residual term; sign may vary by sector and mode
terms.name: Action-linked boundary residual contribution
terms.operator_role: sector boundary residual term tied to the omega scaffold

assumptions: Chirality labels are explicit finite-basis labels.
The coefficient is not fitted to residuals.
limitations: The chirality splitting is still a Level 2 proxy term.
terms.lower_bound_method: Weyl bound from explicit chirality coefficient
terms.name: Chirality splitting contribution

assumptions: Off-diagonal strengths are explicit and nonnegative.
Only same-(k,j,chirality) cross-sector couplings are allowed in Level 2.
limitations: This is the weakest matrix term for analytic upgrade because the present control is finite-basis.
No infinite-basis operator-norm bound is proven here.
terms.lower_bound_method: Gershgorin bound and restricted min-max finite-basis check
terms.name: Sector and boundary off-diagonal coupling
terms.operator_role: symmetric off-diagonal coupling between sectors with matching k, j, chirality

assumptions: The cutoff Lambda^2 is supplied explicitly and defaults to 1/(4*pi).
Dirac-squared input d is nonnegative.
limitations: The heat lift inherits any incompleteness in the lower bound for D^dagger D on the complement.
terms.name: Heat-kernel Hopf lift

assumptions: Profile operators are PSD unless explicit failure tests opt into negative profiles.
The curvature/profile positivity condition is imposed on the complement.
limitations: The full curvature/profile operator from the action is not computed here.
terms.matrix_structure: PSD vector or matrix contribution; negative profiles are rejected by default
terms.name: Positive-semidefinite curvature/profile contribution
terms.operator_role: optional PSD profile contribution on H_perp

assumptions: Exactly three protected zero modes are inserted or projected in the Level 2 scaffold.
The complement is taken orthogonal to that finite coordinate subspace.
limitations: This is the weakest analytic block: dim ker D_twist = 3 is not proven in the full action.
The infinite-dimensional complement separation remains open.
terms.lower_bound_method: min-max restricted complement in finite basis
terms.matrix_structure: coordinate projector in the Level 2 finite basis
terms.name: Zero-mode and complement projector
terms.operator_role: separates three protected zero modes from H_perp in the finite basis

inventory.title: BHSM v1.3A Level 2 H_T Term Inventory

limitations: The report is an analytic-bound development scaffold over DIRAC_PROXY_LEVEL_2.
It does not prove the full no-extra-light-state theorem.
No frozen v1.0/v1.1 predictions or v1.2 action-origin outputs are changed.
next_upgrade_target: Prove the full-action zero-mode/complement decomposition dim ker D_twist = 3 and prove assumptions A1-A6 from the complete operator.
sector_coupling_bound_status: v1.3B adds finite spectral, Frobenius, row-sum, Weyl, and relative-bound estimates. v1.3C adds structured block and generalized relative-bound diagnostics. v1.3D tests uniform-in-k_max stability through k_max=32. v1.3E defines the Hilbert-space/domain assumptions needed to upgrade the scan. The status remains THEOREM_SCAFFOLD, not proven.
title: BHSM v1.3A H_T Analytic-Bound Classification Report

v1_3g_zero_mode_complement_update.claim: BHSM v1.3G formalizes the zero-mode/index and complement-projector scaffold; it does not prove the full index theorem.

v1_3h_diagonal_mirror_update.claim: BHSM v1.3H audits the diagonal complement lower bound and mirror-mode exclusion conditions; it does not prove the full H_T theorem.

v1_3i_mirror_exclusion_update.claim: BHSM v1.3I excludes generated mirror candidates at the chiral-projector scaffold channel, but does not prove the full H_T theorem.

v1_3j_zero_mode_alignment_update.claim: BHSM v1.3J audits formal/coordinate zero-mode alignment and finds partial alignment; it does not prove the full H_T theorem.

v1_3k_sector_labeled_kernel_update.claim: BHSM v1.3K audits formal sector-labeled protected kernel construction and reports that the current Level 2 matrix does not protect the formal up/down kernel coordinates.

v1_3l_formal_kernel_operator_update.claim: Corrected finite-basis formal-kernel Level 2 scaffold; full H_T theorem remains open.

projector.name: P_formal

assumptions: The protected BHSM family labels are the heavy (0,0) sector labels for lepton, up, and down sectors.
The protected chirality convention is chi=-1.
The v1.2 boundary functional supplies the sector labels without using empirical masses.

Mirror-mode exclusion and the full infinite-basis complement split remain proof obligations.
